scoring.description: Score is the percentage of available weighted points that pass. Rules marked not applicable through config are removed from the denominator.

rules.label: README
rules.why: A clear README is the front door for users and contributors.
rules.fix: Add setup, usage, contribution, support, and project status sections to README.md.

rules.label: License
rules.why: A license tells downstream users what they may legally do with the code.
rules.fix: Add an OSI-approved license file such as MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-3-Clause, or MPL-2.0.

rules.label: Contributing guide
rules.why: Maintainers get better issues and pull requests when expectations are documented.
rules.fix: Add CONTRIBUTING.md with local setup, test commands, review expectations, and release notes guidance.

rules.label: Security policy
rules.why: Responsible disclosure needs a private, documented path.
rules.fix: Add SECURITY.md with supported versions, reporting instructions, and response expectations.

rules.label: Code of conduct
rules.why: Community norms reduce ambiguity during difficult interactions.
rules.fix: Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, for example the Contributor Covenant.

rules.label: Changelog
rules.why: Users need a durable place to understand release impact.
rules.fix: Keep CHANGELOG.md with dated release entries and migration notes.

rules.label: Support policy
rules.why: Support boundaries help maintainers avoid turning every request into unpaid consulting.
rules.fix: Add SUPPORT.md describing where to ask questions, what is in scope, and expected response times.

rules.label: Maintainer ownership
rules.why: Clear ownership and review routing make outside contributions easier to triage.
rules.fix: Add MAINTAINERS.md, CODEOWNERS, or GOVERNANCE.md so contributors know who reviews changes and how decisions are made.

rules.label: Continuous integration
rules.why: CI catches regressions before maintainers merge changes.
rules.fix: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting and tests on pushes and pull requests.

rules.label: Tests
rules.why: Tests make review safer and lower the cost of outside contributions.
rules.fix: Add focused tests for public behavior and document the test command.

rules.label: Issue templates
rules.why: Issue templates collect the facts maintainers need to reproduce and triage.
rules.fix: Add bug report and feature request templates under .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/.

rules.label: Pull request template
rules.why: PR templates nudge contributors to include tests, docs, and review context.

rules.label: Dependency update automation
rules.why: Automated dependency updates reduce security and compatibility drift.
rules.fix: Add .github/dependabot.yml for the package ecosystems used in the repository.

rules.label: Static security analysis
signals: Workflow filename containing codeql or security
rules.why: Static analysis finds common vulnerability patterns before releases.
rules.fix: Add a CodeQL or equivalent security scanning workflow.

rules.label: Node package metadata
rules.why: Package metadata makes installation, testing, and release automation discoverable.
rules.fix: Add package.json with name, description, license, scripts, repository, and engines fields.

rules.label: Dependency lockfile
rules.why: Lockfiles make CI and contributor setup reproducible.
rules.fix: Commit the lockfile for application-style projects, or document why this library intentionally omits one.
